rule_title: Fail loud on invalid values — replace ?? fallback with allowlist + throw
rationale: Silent ?? fallbacks on required values mask configuration bugs and production errors. The team rule is fail-loud: validate at the entry point with an explicit allowlist and throw.

rule_title: Throw on missing required config at boot — never swallow with a warning and return
correct_pattern: if (!apiHost) throw new Error('Required config apiHost is missing — refusing to boot.');
rationale: Missing required config must abort the process at startup, not silently degrade. A warning log is invisible in production noise; a thrown error or Sentry capture is not.

rule_title: Never wrap expect() in try/catch — rewrite with proper mocks instead
rationale: A try/catch block around expect() can swallow failed assertions, making broken tests appear green. Rewrite the test to use proper mocks and let Jest fail naturally.

rule_title: Avoid dynamic env-key concatenation — use an explicit Record map instead
rationale: Dynamic environment key construction hides which variables a module depends on and makes static analysis impossible. An explicit Record map makes every dependency visible and type-checked.

rule_title: Use object args for 3+ parameters — never positional lists
rationale: Functions with 3 or more positional parameters are fragile at the call site — argument order is easy to swap silently. Object destructuring is self-documenting and order-independent.

rule_title: One fallback layer only — avoid default params masking a downstream ?? chain
rationale: Combining a default parameter with a downstream ?? fallback creates two sources of truth for the same default. One fallback layer at the boundary makes the intent clear.

rule_title: Do not expand PR scope — open a follow-up ticket for pre-existing unrelated issues
anti_pattern:
// While fixing login bug, also renamed 12 unrelated
// variables and restructured AuthService
correct_pattern:
// Fix only the targeted bug.
// Open a follow-up ticket for unrelated improvements.
rationale: Expanding a PR's scope with unrelated cleanup conflates the change and complicates review and bisect. Open a follow-up ticket instead; keep each PR focused on a single concern.

rule_title: Resolvers must delegate to use cases, never call repositories or domain services directly
rationale: Resolvers belong to the delivery layer and must only orchestrate HTTP/GraphQL concerns. Calling repositories or domain services directly collapses layer boundaries, making the codebase harder to test and maintain.

rule_title: Collect 3+ GraphQL mutation args into a single input object type
rationale: Positional @Args are fragile — silently breaking clients when new arguments are added or reordered. Grouping 3 or more arguments into a dedicated @InputType keeps the API stable and self-documenting.
